exemplar.title: To what extent has the increase in 3-point shooting influenced team offensive efficiency of the Golden State Warriors over the last two decades (2000s and 2010s)?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent has the increase in 3-point shooting influenced team offensive efficiency of the Golden State Warriors over the last two decades (2000s and 2010s)?

subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Well-defined introduction, body sections, and conclusion
Logical progression with only minor slips
weaknesses: Overly long title can overwhelm reader
Inconsistent citation formatting and minimal captions
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand evaluations consistently placed Presentation in band 3: the exploration is coherent, well organized, and mostly concise. Minor logical slips, inconsistent citations and some repetition prevent a band 4, so a band 3 score (3 / 4) accurately reflects the quality.

strengths: Appropriate use of symbols and multiple representations
Effective integration of tables and charts into the narrative
weaknesses: Missing units in tables and unclear axis labels on graphs
Unreadable placeholder entries in some tables
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Communication is clear and mostly consistent, with effective use of multiple representations, matching band 3. Occasional notation slips, missing units, and labeling errors prevent band 4, so 3 / 4 is justified.

strengths: Topic choice and variable selection show significant independent thinking
Personal passion for basketball clearly motivates the exploration
Data segmentation by decade reflects student initiative
weaknesses: Methods remain conventional without exploring alternative models
Limited creative extension beyond standard correlation/regression
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates a clear personal approach and significant independent thinking (band 2), but creativity and initiative are not at the âoutstandingâ level required for band 3, so a score of 2 / 3 is appropriate.

strengths: Some links are made between results and research question
weaknesses: Reflection remains descriptive rather than critical
No thorough examination of assumptions or p-values
Limited discussion of how proposed new variables alter methodology
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection is present but only at a basic level with limited critique, matching band 1. There is insufficient depth or critical analysis for bands 2 or 3, so 1 / 3 is awarded.

strengths: Appropriate use of correlation and regression at HL level
Mathematics is mostly correct with only minor transcription or rounding errors
Good understanding of statistical concepts like RÂ²
weaknesses: No advanced or sophisticated mathematical methods beyond basic statistics
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Use of Mathematics aligns with band 3: relevant HL mathematics is applied correctly in parts. Minor errors and lack of sophistication preclude band 4 or higher, so a score of 3 / 6 accurately reflects performance.

strandSelections.reasoning: Choosing specific statistics (ORTG, TS%), deciding to split the data by decade, and extending the investigation to individual positions show the studentâs own decision-making beyond textbook examples.  However, the approach relies on standard correlation/regression without exploring alternative models or deeper diagnostics, so evidence is âsignificantâ rather than âoutstanding.â

Explanations accompanying statistical techniques show the student understands what correlation, slope and RÂ² represent, yet the discussion lacks depth (no comment on p-values, residual patterns).  Understanding is therefore âgood in partsâ rather than thorough, meriting band 3.

Numerical work (averages, correlation values, slopes) is largely correct; small transcription and rounding errors are present but do not significantly alter conclusions.  This aligns with âmathematics is mostly correct,â which is characteristic of band 3.

Almost all content directly addresses the research question; tables, correlations and regressions are relevant.  Nevertheless, lengthy narrative explanations of basic basketball context and repeated formulae create some unnecessary bulk, so the work cannot be described as âhighly concise.â  Band 3 (ârelevant and mostly conciseâ) best matches the quality shown.

The student employs appropriate symbols (Î£, \bar{x}, r), writes regression in y = mx + c form, and distinguishes variables.  There are occasional notation slipsâpercent signs used as variable names, inconsistent bold formatting, missing bracketsâwhich prevent full consistency, but overall mathematical language is sound and mainly correct, fitting band 3.

Explanations usually accompany calculations, and figure labels are present, so the argument is understandable.  Inconsistencies such as switching between ORG/ORTG and rounding steps inside formulae reduce absolute clarity, yet the overall communication remains clear and suffers only minor lapses, aligning with band 3.

Correlation coefficients, linear regressions and percentage change calculations are solidly within the HL syllabus and are appropriate to the question.  The mathematics is applied directly to real data, demonstrating relevance; however, the methods are not employed at a sophisticated or advanced level, so band 3 is the best match.

Data are communicated through tables, multi-series line charts, scatter plots with regression lines, and comparative bar charts.  These multiple representations genuinely aid interpretation and are integrated into the text, fulfilling the descriptor of âeffective useâ characteristic of band 3.  Sophistication (dynamic modelling, interactive graphics) is absent, so band 4 is not justified.

Reflection is present mainly in descriptive statements such as âa correlation of 0.7048 is strong.â  The student notes limitations (other factors like turnovers) but does not probe them quantitatively.  This constitutes âlimited reflection,â so band 1 is appropriate.

While applying Pearson correlation and basic regression is appropriate, the mathematical methods are conventional and there is little evidence of creative extensions (e.g. non-linear modelling, residual analysis).  Some initiative is present in assembling and segmenting data, thus band 1 (âsome creativity or initiativeâ) is best suited.

The exploration follows a clear pathâintroduction, data presentation, analysis for two decades, player-level analysis, and a conclusionâso the reader can follow the argument without back-tracking.  Minor logical slips (e.g. repeating the definition of variables, mixing decade labels) do not seriously impede understanding, but they prevent the work from feeling âexcellent.â  Overall, coherence and development are evident and consistent with band 3.

Critical analysis is restricted to comparing numerical values and indicating that RÂ² is ânot that high.â  There is no thorough examination of assumptions, data quality, or alternative interpretations.  Hence the work fits the âbasic analysis with limited critiqueâ descriptor of band 1.

The student does link mathematical findings back to the research question, but connections remain surface-level (e.g. âeven a 1 percent increase can decide a gameâ) and do not explore how the mathematics deepens conceptual understanding.  Therefore, a limited connection (band 1) is justified.

Headings, numbered tables, and figures give the report a recognisable structure; the introduction states a question, body sections treat data and methods, and a concluding section summarises findings.  Cross-references are occasionally missing and captioning is sometimes minimal, but the separation of sections is clear enough to merit the âwell organisedâ descriptor of band 3 rather than merely âadequate.â

The narrative makes clear that the topic stems from the studentâs long-standing interest in basketball, and this personal motivation shapes the variables selected and the comparisons made.  This constitutes a âclear personal approach,â yet it does not reach the level of originality or insight that would âenhance understandingâ in the sense required for band 3.

annotations.comment: The research question is clearly stated and provides a focused aim, guiding the structure of the entire exploration effectively.
The title is exceptionally long, which can overwhelm the reader. Consider shortening it to focus on key terms and maintain engagement.
Citation markers are inconsistently formatted (e.g., HTML tags visible). Ensure superscripts and references follow a consistent academic style.
The rationale vividly demonstrates personal passion and motivation, showing genuine engagement with the topic.
The rationale mixes personal narrative with methodological context without clear separation. Consider structuring it into distinct paragraphs for interest and methods.
Table 1 presents useful data but lacks unit annotations (e.g., 3PA per game). Adding units will improve clarity and interpretability.
While you describe TS%, the formula is only in text. Include an explicit equation such as $$TS\% = \frac{Points}{2\times FGA + 0.475\ FT} \times100$$ for precision.
The notation for the mean of 3PA is given as $$\overline{10}$$ rather than the standard $$\bar{x}$$. Use consistent mathematical symbols.
The average TS% calculation is shown but lacks interpretation. Briefly comment on what the average implies about the teamâs performance.
The Pearson formula is presented but the numerical denominator under the square root is omitted. Show the substitution for full transparency.
The line chart lacks clearly labeled axes and a legend explaining colors. Adding these will make the representation more accessible.
Table 6 appears to contain placeholder symbols and is unreadable. Replace with correct values or remove if unnecessary.
Multiple player graphs are presented without individual captions or interpretation. Add descriptive captions to guide the reader.
Your final reflection describes general next steps but could be strengthened by proposing a specific follow-up investigation.
Variable symbols alternate between boldface and normal (e.g. \(\mathbf{x}\) vs \(x\)). Maintain consistent notation throughout.
Rather than repeating the same summation for \(\Sigma(x_i-\bar{x})^2\), reference the previous result to improve conciseness.
The variable definition list uses an inline \(\bullet\) symbol inconsistently within math mode. Use plain text or consistent LaTeX formatting.
You list R-squared values without interpreting their magnitude. Discuss what an RÂ² around 0.497 implies about model fit and unexplained variance.
The 2010s graph is visually clear but missing axis labels and a legend explaining data series. Please add them.
The header for the 2010s mean calculation mistakenly states â2000â2010â instead of â2010â2020â. Correct to avoid confusion.
The comparative percent-change calculation is shown without intermediate steps. Display the full calculation to strengthen mathematical communication.
The text describes increased correlations but lacks critical analysis of statistical significance. Consider discussing p-values or confidence intervals.
Your choice of shooting efficiency metric for individual players is appropriate, but you should explain why this metric best reflects shooting performance.
Table 3âs header âShooting Effâ is ambiguous. Specify whether this refers to TS%, FG%, or another measure.
The individual correlation coefficient (0.696) is reported without showing its derivation. Include the calculation steps or reference where it appears.
The conclusion mentions adding variables like turnovers but lacks discussion of how these would alter the analysis methodologically. Critically reflect on study limitations.
